priorClosureDisposition: previous through-fourth-gate promotion claims were overstated and effectively false
actualDevianceDisposition: actual deviance was unknown before re-review; no blocking fourth-gate deviance is detected by the mapped proof families and acceptance criteria
fifthGateWorkPosture: active and open; fifth-gate source-to-shares work may proceed after fourth-gate promoted closure but is not claimed closed by this proof

criteria.label: Conversations, chat, execution, and runs surfaces remain first-class application systems
criteria.detail: Application, conversation, and runs proof families keep retained interfaces inside the V26 Bitcode system.

criteria.label: Retained systems are specified as Bitcode V26 systems rather than adjacent subsystems
criteria.detail: Application composition, inference records, and retained-package roles bind retained systems into Bitcode V26.

criteria.label: Conversations operate as a fullscreen application mode rather than a separate product reservoir
criteria.detail: The conversations continuity proof covers the fullscreen application-mode carrier and route/API bindings.

criteria.label: Execution and deliverable master-detail patterns are ported inward to /application
criteria.detail: Application composition and runs proof families cover the inward master-detail transaction/workspace posture.

criteria.label: Prompt abstraction is the direct source for retained prompt text
criteria.detail: The prompt-system totality proof covers PromptPart, Prompt, PromptExecution, and public prompt-boundary routing.

criteria.label: Persistence ownership across Supabase, ORM, storage-facing APIs, and /edgetimes is explicit
criteria.detail: The persistence/schema proof covers the V26 migration, typed clients, ORM/query carriers, and /edgetimes witnesses.

criteria.label: Migrations, schema contracts, ORM/query carriers, and generated types have retained-system obligations
criteria.detail: The persistence/schema proof keeps schema, generated types, and route witnesses in the fourth-gate proof set.

criteria.label: Retained package roles and boundaries are explicit
criteria.detail: The retained-package admissibility proof requires role, write-boundary, rationale, proof-obligation, and source witnesses.

criteria.label: Deliverable, run, and pipeline meaning are explicit under V26
criteria.detail: Runs/pipelines and inference records keep retained execution carriers tied to Bitcode activity, AssetPack, and written-asset semantics.

criteria.label: Old-world tools and agents are Bitcode-purposed with prompt ownership and reader-first scope
criteria.detail: Prompt, inference, and retained-package proofs keep old-world ports bounded to Bitcode roles such as need ingestion and source-attributed support.

criteria.label: Git/GitHub-based settle-write carries the initial testnet-ready asset settlement posture
criteria.detail: Runs/pipelines and retained-package admission keep Git/GitHub as the initial connected-interface delivery and settle-write boundary.

criteria.label: Retained executions compatibility APIs are explicit promotion-boundary owners
criteria.detail: The runs/pipelines proof names retained execution, history, VCS, template, and deliverable route boundaries.

criteria.label: Canonical auxillary APIs are explicit active owners rather than latent pane dependencies
criteria.detail: Application and runs/activity proof inputs include auxillary API, notification, profile, connection, BTD, usage, transaction, and key carriers.

criteria.label: Executions and orbitals compatibility routes teach executions and auxillaries as merged-world targets
criteria.detail: Application composition plus runs/activity proofing keeps compatibility routes bounded while /application and auxillaries carry the canonical topology.

criteria.label: Transactions, execution events, and notifications share one typed Bitcode activity vocabulary
criteria.detail: Activity and transaction carriers are included in the application and runs proof basis rather than separate product semantics.

criteria.label: Fourth-gate proof obligations are assigned to generated proof families
criteria.detail: Generated proof families cover conversations, runs/pipelines, persistence/schema, prompts, inference records, and retained packages.

criteria.label: Procedural acceptance closes only after reopened claims are re-reviewed and no blocking fourth-gate deviance remains
criteria.detail: The reopened fourth-gate acceptance claim has been re-reviewed against every material criterion and no blocking fourth-gate deviance is detected.
